description_long: This filing cabinet is constructed from brown wooden planks, featuring a simple, rustic design reminiscent of old-fashioned storage furniture. The front face is divided by two horizontal black bands, likely representing structural reinforcements or drawer separations. A hexagonal black handle is attached to the right side of the upper section, suggesting a single access point for opening. The top edge is uneven, with three notched cutouts, adding a handcrafted or medieval touch. The sides are flat and show the thickness of the wood, while the back is plain with matching horizontal bands. The overall appearance is robust and utilitarian, suitable for secure storage in a functional environment.
description: A rustic brown wooden filing cabinet with black horizontal bands and a hexagonal handle, featuring notched cutouts along the top edge.
description_view_0: Front view shows the notched top, two black bands, and a hexagonal handle on the right.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the cabinet's thickness and the protruding handle.
description_view_2: Back view displays a plain surface with two black bands and no handle or notches.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the cabinet's thickness without the handle.
